reason: The statement applies because the text portrays Yahweh as delivering the children of Israel into the hands of the Midianites due to their disobedience, causing suffering and hardship. The text also depicts Yahweh sending a prophet and an angel to intervene and guide Gideon in delivering Israel from the Midianites through warfare and miraculous signs. The narrative demonstrates Yahweh's role in orchestrating violence and vengeance against the oppressors of Israel, involving multiple people and nations, not just a handful.
